login.title: Moodly
login.subtitle: Sign in to your account
login.welcomeMessage: We're here to help you notice patterns and track how you feel — at your own pace, without judgement.
login.disclaimer: Moodly is a self-assessment and wellness tracking tool, not a medical device. It does not diagnose, treat, or prevent any condition. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider for mental health concerns.
login.privacyNotice: Your data stays on your device. We do not store your test results, entries, or personal information on our servers.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noEntries: No entries yet — start when you feel ready. Every small step helps.
dashboard.noTrendData: No entries yet — your data will appear here once you start tracking.
dashboard.noTestData: No test results yet — take your first test when you feel ready.
dashboard.recentEntry: You already logged this parameter {{minutes}} min ago
dashboard.recentEntry_1: You already logged this parameter 1 min ago
dashboard.gratitudePlaceholder: What are you grateful for today?
dashboard.gratitudeEmpty: No entries yet — write down something small you're grateful for.
dashboard.gratitudePageSubtitle: A small, regular habit of noticing what's good.

onboarding.welcomeDesc: We're here to help you notice patterns in how you feel — so you can understand yourself better and have more meaningful conversations with your therapist.
onboarding.startDesc: There's no pressure to do everything at once. You can explore at your own pace and come back anytime.
onboarding.chooseTitle: What would you like to do?

testDetail.reviewDesc: Tap any question to change your answer
testDetail.exitConfirmDesc: You can always come back and continue where you left off.

testResults.title: Test Results
testResults.noResults: No results yet — take your first test when you feel ready.
testResults.disclaimer: This test is an educational self-assessment tool. It is not a medical diagnosis and does not replace professional evaluation. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider for mental health concerns.

reports.title: Reports
reports.noReports: No exports yet — your data is ready when you are.

breathing.title: Breathing Exercise
breathing.subtitle: Calm your anxiety creature with deep breathing
breathing.description478: The 4-7-8 breathing technique helps activate your parasympathetic nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety. Complete 4 cycles to see your creature calm down.
breathing.descriptionBox: Box breathing (4-4-4) balances your nervous system by equalising inhale, hold, exhale, and hold. A gentle technique for general relaxation.
breathing.descriptionQuick: The Quick Calm technique uses a short inhale and long exhale to rapidly activate the parasympathetic response. Best for moments of acute anxiety or panic.
breathing.sessionComplete: You breathed for {{duration}} seconds. Your creature feels calmer.
breathing.step1: Press Start and follow the breathing circle
breathing.step2: Inhale as the circle expands (4 seconds)
breathing.step3: Hold your breath while the circle pauses (7 seconds)
breathing.step4: Exhale slowly as the circle contracts (8 seconds)

crisis.bodyUrgent: Your responses indicate you may be having thoughts of self-harm. Please reach out to a crisis helpline right now.
crisis.bodyCritical: Your responses indicate a plan for self-harm. Emergency intervention is needed immediately.

contentWarning.title: The following questions may be difficult
contentWarning.body: These questions help us better understand your situation. If you are in crisis, please contact a helpline before proceeding.

forgotPassword.title: Forgot Password
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your email and we'll send you a reset link
forgotPassword.sent: If this email is registered, a reset link has been sent.

resetPassword.title: Set New Password
resetPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters

distortions.title: Cognitive Distortions
distortions.subtitle: Learn to recognize distorted thinking patterns in your own thoughts.

allOrNothing.definition: Seeing things in only two extremes — perfect or a total failure — with no middle ground.
allOrNothing.example: "I made one mistake in the presentation, so the whole thing was a disaster."
allOrNothing.reframe: "One part didn't go perfectly, but most of it went well — that doesn't erase the rest."

overgeneralization.definition: Taking one negative event and treating it as a permanent, never-ending pattern.
overgeneralization.example: "My friend didn't text back — nobody ever wants to talk to me."
overgeneralization.reframe: "One person being busy right now doesn't mean this always happens."

mentalFilter.definition: Picking out a single negative detail and dwelling on it so much that your whole view of the situation darkens — like a drop of ink coloring an entire glass of water.
mentalFilter.example: "The meeting had one awkward moment, so the whole thing felt like a failure."
mentalFilter.reframe: "Most of the meeting actually went smoothly — I'm zooming in on the one rough moment."

discountingPositive.definition: Insisting that your good qualities or achievements don't count.
discountingPositive.example: "I only did well because the test was easy, not because I'm capable."
discountingPositive.reframe: "I prepared for this and it paid off — that's a real accomplishment."

jumpingToConclusions.definition: Assuming you know what others think (mind reading) or predicting the worst outcome (fortune telling) without evidence.
jumpingToConclusions.example: "She looked at her phone during our chat — she must think I'm boring."
jumpingToConclusions.reframe: "I don't actually know what she was thinking — there could be many reasons."

magnification.definition: Blowing problems out of proportion, or shrinking your own strengths down to nothing.
magnification.example: "I forgot one detail in the report — this could ruin my whole career."
magnification.reframe: "It's a small error I can correct — it's not going to define my career."

emotionalReasoning.definition: Assuming your feelings reflect the facts: 'I feel it, so it must be true.'
emotionalReasoning.example: "I feel like a failure, so I must actually be one."
emotionalReasoning.reframe: "Feeling like a failure right now doesn't mean I am one — feelings aren't proof."

shouldStatements.definition: Criticizing yourself or others with rigid 'should,' 'must,' or 'ought to' rules.
shouldStatements.example: "I should never make mistakes at work."
shouldStatements.reframe: "I'm allowed to make mistakes — that's how people learn and improve."

labeling.definition: Attaching a harsh, global label to yourself or someone else based on one incident.
labeling.example: "I missed the deadline — I'm such a failure."
labeling.reframe: "I missed one deadline — that's a single event, not who I am."

personalization.definition: Blaming yourself for events that were largely outside your control.
personalization.example: "My team missed the target — it must be entirely my fault."
personalization.reframe: "Many factors contributed to this — I'm not the only cause."

distortionsQuiz.intro: Read the thought below and guess which cognitive distortion it shows.
distortionsQuiz.question: Which distortion is this?
distortionsQuiz.scoreSummary: {{score}} of {{total}} — this takes practice, keep at it.
distortionsQuiz.q1: Alex got one question wrong on the exam and thought, "I totally failed."
distortionsQuiz.q2: After being turned down for one job, Maria decided she'd never get hired anywhere.
distortionsQuiz.q3: The trip had eight great days and one rainy afternoon — but all Sam remembers is the rain.
distortionsQuiz.q4: Jamie got a compliment on the report and thought, "They're just being polite, it wasn't actually good."
distortionsQuiz.q5: A coworker didn't say hello in the hallway, and Priya immediately assumed he was angry with her.
distortionsQuiz.q6: Leo spilled coffee on his shirt before a meeting and thought his whole career was ruined.
distortionsQuiz.q7: Nina felt anxious about the flight, so she concluded the plane must be unsafe.
distortionsQuiz.q8: Tom kept telling himself, "I should always know the right answer immediately."
distortionsQuiz.q9: After forgetting a friend's birthday, Dana thought, "I'm a terrible friend."
distortionsQuiz.q10: When the team project got a low grade, Ben assumed it was entirely his fault, even though three people worked on it.
distortionsQuiz.q11: If Chloe doesn't get a perfect score, she considers the whole effort worthless.
distortionsQuiz.q12: Before even asking, Marcus was sure his boss would say no to his vacation request.
distortionsQuiz.q13: One awkward date convinced Yuki that she's simply bad at relationships.
distortionsQuiz.q14: Oliver believes everyone around him should always be on time, no exceptions.
distortionsQuiz.q15: Despite five positive reviews, all Ravi can think about is the one critical comment.

sleepHygiene.title: Sleep Hygiene
sleepHygiene.subtitle: A nightly checklist to build habits that support better sleep.
sleepHygiene.empty: No nights logged yet — start whenever you're ready.

items.noCaffeine: No caffeine in the last 6 hours
items.noScreens: No screens in the last 30–60 minutes
items.consistentBedtime: Went to bed around my usual time
items.darkQuietCool: Room was dark, cool, and quiet
items.noLateMeal: No heavy or late meal close to bedtime

recommendation.welcomeBreathing: Welcome! Try a quick breathing exercise to settle in.
recommendation.highAnxiety: I see you're feeling anxious. Would you like a short breathing exercise?
recommendation.lowMood: Things feel tough right now. Want to write down something you're grateful for?
recommendation.poorSleep: Sleep has been rough lately. Want to check your sleep hygiene?
recommendation.lowEnergy: You're running low on energy. A quick breathing exercise might help.
recommendation.anxietyFromTest: Your last test shows elevated anxiety. A breathing exercise could help.
recommendation.lowMoodFromTest: Your last test shows low mood. Try a gratitude journal entry.
recommendation.defaultBreathing: Take a moment for yourself with a breathing exercise.
